chunk_summary: Guidelines for assessing dehydration and managing fluids in children, including IMNCI plans, ORS, IV fluids, zinc supplementation, and caregiver education.
learning_objectives: Understand IMNCI dehydration management plans (A, B, C) for children.
Learn fluid resuscitation protocols for severe dehydration using IV isotonic fluids.
Recognize signs of dehydration and reassess fluid needs after each intervention.
Educate caregivers on home-based ORS use and warning signs requiring escalation.
key_facts: Plan A: ORS 50–100 mL after each stool for children <2 years; 100–200 mL for 2–10 years.
Plan B: ORS 75 mL/kg over 4 hours with observation; pause for vomiting and restart with small sips.
Plan C: IV isotonic fluids (RL or 0.9% NS) 100 mL/kg; infants: 30 mL/kg in 1 hour, 70 mL/kg in 5 hours; older children: 30 mL/kg in 30 minutes, 70 mL/kg in 2.5 hours.
Zinc supplementation: 10 mg/day for 2–6 months; 20 mg/day for older infants and children for 14 days.
Signs of dehydration: lethargy, sunken eyes, thirst, poor skin pinch, low urine output, altered sensorium.
reference_quote: For acute watery diarrhea, follow IMNCI dehydration plans and treat vomiting with small frequent sips. Plan A (no dehydration) is home-based ORS after each loose stool plus continued feeding.
document_key_topics: Caregiver education on hydration and warning signs
